content.full_text:
• 
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 15, 1988 
Beverly Anglin, Filing Ofi':!cer 
Pleasanton Unified School District 
4665 Bernal Avenue 
Pleasanton, California 94566-7498 
Dear Ms. Anglin: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-200 
You have requested advice concerning the conflict of 
interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act") .y 
FACTS 
The Amador/Pleasanton school District is being reorganized 
and will become the Pleasanton Unified school District. 
The 
Amador/Pleasanton School District's conflict of interest code 
will terminate as of July 1, 1988. 
Since it is no longer a 
multi-county agency, the Fair Political Practices Commission 
will no longer be the code reviewing body for this school 
district. 
(Section 82011 (b) .) 
YGovernment Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations Section 18000, et seg. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of 
Regulations. 
Because you are seeking clarification of the filing 
obligations of persons who are not specifically identified, and 
who have not authorized you to seek advice on their behalf, this 
letter constitutes informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 
18329 (c) . 


Beverly Anglin 
Page Two 
July 15, 1988 
The jurisdiction of the new Pleasanton Unified School 
District is wholly within Alameda County which is the new code 
reviewing body. 
You advised me that Alameda County recently 
adopted a conflict of interest code for this new school district. 
QUESTIONS 
1. 
What are the filing obligations for the eight board 
members retiring from board service? 
2. 
What are the filing'obligations for the two board 
members who will serve on the new Pleasanton Unified District? 
CONCLUSIONS 
1. The eight board members who are retiring from board 
service as of July I, 1988, will be required to file a leaving 
office statement of economic interests (Form 730) within 30 days 
of leaving office. 
The statements are due on August 1, 1988.£1 
2. The two board members who will serve on the new 
Pleasanton Unified School District Board must file leaving 
• office statements of economic interests (Form 730) within 30 
days of the effective date of the district's conflict of 
interest code. 
These statements are due by August I, 1988.£1 
The new conflict of interest code for the Pleasanton Unified 
School District will become effective 30 days after the date of 
approval. 
Each member sitting of the new district's board will 
be required to file an initial statement within 30 days after 
the effective date of the conflict of interest code. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act requires each state or local government agency to 
adopt a conflict of interest code within six months after it 
comes into existence. 
The conflict of interest code requires 
designated employees to disclose certain investments, interests 
in real property, business positions and income. 
(Section 
87303.) 
£lWhen the deadline for filing a statement falls on a 
Saturday, Sunday or official state holiday, the deadline is 
extended to the next regular business day. 
(Regulation 18116). 
Since the filing deadline of July 30 falls on a Saturday, the 
deadline is extended. 


• 
Beverly Anglin 
Page Three 
July 15, 1988 
Each designated employee must file an initial 
within 30 days after the effective date of the confict of 
interest code. 
Designated employees also must file annual 
statements. 
For agencies which have adopted the standard 
conflict of interest code (Regulation ~8730), annual statements 
are due by April 1st each year. 
Upon leaving office each 
designated employee must file a leaving office statement within 
30 days. (Section 87303.) 
The Fair Political Practices Commission is the code reviewing 
body for multi-county agendies. 
The board of supervisors is the 
code reviewing body for any local government agency, other than 
a city agency, with jurisdiction who'lly within the county. 
(Section 82011(b).) 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have additional 
questions regarding this matter. 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
i2,it. (! i/ Xii1(~' 
Al~ce HugheS' 
Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division 


• 
BOARDS OF TRUSTEES 
AMADOR 
Amador-Pleasanton Public 
4665 Bernal Avenue • Pleasanton, CA 94566-7498 
(415) 462-5500 
.. 
May 23, 1988 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Gentlemen: 
As of July 1, 1988, the Amador/Pleasanton School 
Districts will become the Pleasanton Unified District. 
There will be eight of the ten board members of these 
two boards retiring from board service. 
Do they each 
fill ocit a "Leaving Office Statement"? 
If so, would 
you please send me 8 such statements. 
Also, would the 
two board members who will serve on the new Pleasanton 
Unified Board fill out "Initial Statements" for the 
new board, as well as the three new members. 
If so, 
please send me the necessary forms for this action. 
BA 
I await your direction in this matter. 
Sincerely, 
/i 
'0.£~ (£J,~~~ 
\ 
BEVERLY ANGlIN 
0 
Filing Officer for the 
Amador/Pleasanton Districts 
Donald C. Biddie, President 
David C. Melander, Clerk 
Gail Fairfield 
Juanita Haugen 
LB. Pereira 
PLEASANTON 
Charles EddInger 
Nancy Haw/rey 
Dr. L Bruce Merrill 
SUPERINTENDENT 
Dr. Bill J, James 


May 23, 1988 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Gentlemen: 
As of July 1, 1988, the Amador/Pleasanton School 
Districts will become the Pleasanton Unified District. 
There will be eight of the ten board members of these 
two boards retiring from board service. 
Do they each 
fill out a "Leaving Office Statement"? 
If so, would 
you please send me 8 such statements. 
Also, would the 
two board members who will serve on the new Pleasanton 
Unified Board fill out "Initial Statements" for the 
new board, as well as the three new members. 
If so, 
please send me the necessary forms for this action. 
BA 
I await your direction in this matter. 
Amador 
BEVERLY 
Filing Officer for the 
Amador/Pleasanton Districts
